title: Project Report: Helpdesk Ticket Simulation Lab — IT Support / Helpdesk
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Process 20 simulated tickets: triage, troubleshoot, document resolution, calculate SLA metrics.
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Beginner)*

**Helpdesk Ticket Simulation Lab** is a beginner-level end-to-end project for IT Support / Helpdesk. Duration: 1–2 weeks. Process 20 simulated tickets: triage, troubleshoot, document resolution, calculate SLA metrics. Tools: Ticketing template, KB template.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Triage 20 tickets by priority
Objective: Document resolution steps
Objective: Calculate average resolution time
Objective: Create KB article
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
IT Support / Helpdesk portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a IT Support / Helpdesk resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a IT Support / Helpdesk resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Triage
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Triage** *(Level: Beginner)*

Priority matrix. Complete these tasks: P1-P4 classification; Assignment. Deliverable for this phase: Triage spreadsheet.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] P1-P4 classification
- [ ] Assignment
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### Where to use it

### Real use example

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Beginner)*

Submit all final deliverables: 20 ticket resolutions; 3 KB articles; SLA report. Skills demonstrated: Troubleshooting, Documentation, SLA. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: 20 ticket resolutions
Deliverable: 3 KB articles
Deliverable: SLA report
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits 20 ticket resolutions and passes the rubric.
key_takeaways: Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.
